title: Eskom Debt Relief Programme — Conditions and Milestones
description: Formalises the ZAR 254 billion government debt relief package for Eskom over three years (FY2023-FY2025), subject to quarterly performance milestones on Energy Availability Factor, load shedding reduction, and unbundling progress.
notes: Year 1 and Year 2 tranches disbursed. EAF improved from 54% to 62%. Unbundling of transmission entity (NTD) progressing. Year 3 tranche conditional on EAF reaching 65%.

title: Eskom Unbundling — Transmission Entity Separation
issuedBy: Department of Public Enterprises and Economic Planning
description: Directs Eskom to complete the legal and operational separation of the National Transmission and Dispatch Company (NTDC) as an independent subsidiary by December 2025, enabling open access to the transmission grid for independent power producers.
notes: NTDC incorporated as subsidiary. Grid Code amendments in progress. NERSA licensing of NTDC expected Q3 2025.

title: Transnet Operational Recovery Plan — Performance Conditions
description: Requires Transnet to implement the Freight Rail Recovery Plan targeting 230 million tonnes per annum by FY2026, with quarterly reporting on locomotive availability, cable theft incidents, and port throughput metrics.
notes: FY2024 freight volumes reached 195 Mtpa, up from 155 Mtpa in FY2023. Locomotive availability improved to 52%. Cable theft incidents reduced by 35% following security deployment.

title: SAA Strategic Equity Partnership — Transaction Completion
issuedBy: Department of Public Enterprises and Economic Planning
description: Directs SAA management to conclude the Takatso Consortium strategic equity partnership transaction by December 2024, or present alternative strategic options to the shareholder by January 2025.
notes: Takatso transaction not concluded by deadline. SAA presented alternative options to shareholder in February 2025. New transaction timeline under review.

title: Denel Recapitalisation and Turnaround Conditions
description: Approves ZAR 3.4 billion recapitalisation of Denel subject to implementation of a turnaround plan including headcount reduction, disposal of non-core assets, and achievement of ZAR 4 billion revenue target by FY2025.
notes: Recapitalisation funds disbursed. Headcount reduced from 4,800 to 3,500. Revenue target of ZAR 4.5 billion achieved in FY2024. Non-core asset disposals in progress.

title: SOE Governance and Board Effectiveness Framework
description: Establishes a standardised board effectiveness assessment framework for all SAMSOC portfolio SOEs, requiring annual board evaluations, mandatory director induction programmes, and quarterly board performance reporting to SAMSOC.
notes: Board effectiveness assessments completed for Eskom, Transnet, and SANRAL. SAA, Denel, and PRASA assessments scheduled for Q2 2025.
